name: Old Fashioned Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup cold butter, cut into small pieces
8 large Granny Smith apples (about 4 pounds), peeled, cored and cut into 1/2 inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring cider to boil and reduce to about 1/2 cup. Cool for about 20 minutes.
2. Crust: Mix together cider vinegar and water in a bowl. Process flour and butter until coarse crumbs form. Add the water-cider mixture and process just until the dough leaves the side of the bowl. Form into two balls, wrap in plastic, and chill for 30-45 minutes.
3. Filling: In a bowl, mix together sugar, flour, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Add apples and toss to coat well. Sprinkle reduced apple cider over the filling and toss to coat well.
4. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll out half of the dough to a 12-inch circle. Place in a 9-inch pie plate. Spoon the apple mixture into the shell, mounding high in the center. Roll out remaining dough and place over filling.
6. Press edges together and roll up the pie, forming an even rim. Flute decoratively.
7. Brush the top of the pastry with water and sprinkle with 1 1/2 tablespoons sugar.
8. Cut slits in the top for steam to escape. Place on a baking sheet and bake at 425°F (220°C) for 30 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350°F (180°C) and continue baking for 45 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and apples are bubbly. If the top crust browns too quickly, cover loosely with foil.
9. Cool on a wire rack before serving warm.
